data.narrative:
Kipu Quantum is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised approximately $14.5M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a fragmented comp universe of 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private companies, including later-stage players such as IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The dataset contains 7 same-sector peers, but filtering for matching stage, adjacent development phase, or comparable valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold for reliable median derivation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the wide dispersion in maturity and capital requirements across quantum platforms.

The absence of sufficient same-stage or near-stage comparables reflects a structural gap in the quantum computing funding landscape: most documented peers are either public or significantly later-stage private companies. This forces reliance on alternative valuation anchors not supplied in the current dataset—such as revenue multiples, burn-rate analysis, or narrative-driven venture method—to establish a defensible range. Without triangulated results from applicable methods, a point estimate cannot be supported. Any valuation recommendation requires either: (1) acquisition of additional seed-stage quantum or deep-tech infrastructure comps, or (2) explicit use of venture method assumptions around exit scenarios and dilution.
